steps.title: Spot the two-part pattern
steps.content: The decisive clue is that the passage pairs two findings about black-furred wolves: they're both more common in high-distemper areas AND more likely to be immune to distemper than gray-furred wolves.

steps.title: Connect the pattern to the gene variant
steps.content: If the gene variant is linked to immunity, it explains both findings: immune black wolves survive outbreaks and increase in frequency in disease-prone areas. The other choices either ignore the immunity evidence, contradict the observed pattern, or add unsupported claims. That matches choice B — B is correct.
